title: vatica oblongifolia subsp. elliptifolia
intro: Vatica oblongifolia subsp. elliptifolia, also known as the Borneo ironwood, is a subspecies of tree native to the tropical forests of Borneo. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been shown to have various health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. The health benefits of Vatica oblongifolia subsp. elliptifolia include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of Vatica oblongifolia subsp. elliptifolia typically involve extracts from the tree's bark or leaves, which can be consumed as teas, tinctures, or capsules, and have been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
